title: First unusual deaths reported in Mongbwalu
description: Healthcare worker deaths from hemorrhagic illness noted retrospectively. Suspected index case onset.

title: WHO alerted to high-mortality outbreak
description: Unknown illness with high mortality reported in Mongbwalu health zone, Ituri Province.

title: Bundibugyo virus laboratory confirmed
description: 8 of 13 blood samples positive for Bundibugyo ebolavirus at INRB Kinshasa.

title: DRC declares Ebola outbreak
description: Government officially declares Bundibugyo virus disease outbreak. Uganda also declares outbreak same day.

title: WHO declares PHEIC
description: WHO Director-General declares Public Health Emergency of International Concern. No approved vaccines exist for Bundibugyo strain.

title: Case confirmed in Kinshasa
description: Confirmed case detected in capital; US physician evacuated to Germany after testing positive.

title: WHO SitRep 01 published
description: First weekly external situation report: 8 confirmed, 393 suspected, 105 deaths across 7 health zones.

title: US commits $13 million in aid
description: US State Department announces emergency funding. CDC deploys 25 staff already in DRC.

title: UK allocates £20 million
description: UK government announces emergency aid package for Ebola response in DRC and Uganda.

title: WHO upgrades DRC risk to Very High
description: National risk level raised from High to Very High. 13 health zones affected across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu.

title: WHO SitRep 02 published
description: Cases rise to 105 confirmed, 495 suspected, 148 deaths. Uganda reports 7 confirmed cases, 1 death.

title: CDC imposes US travel restrictions
description: 30-day entry ban on travelers from DRC, Uganda, South Sudan; exemptions for US citizens/permanent residents.

title: Uganda closes DRC border
description: National Task Force orders immediate temporary border closure (4 weeks). Exemptions for Ebola response teams, food/cargo. Mandatory 21-day quarantine for entrants.

title: UN launches $15.8M emergency appeal
description: Three-month plan (May-August). $3.1M mobilized, $12.7M gap. Focus: case management, IPC, surveillance, WASH, refugees.

title: Uganda tightens cross-border controls
description: Schools in border districts ordered to enforce screening; media mandated 30+ min daily Ebola sensitization.

title: Cases near 1,000 as containment efforts intensify
description: DRC reports 977+ suspected cases, 228+ suspected deaths (CFR ~14.3%). Four treatment centers attacked in Ituri.

title: IsraAID launches emergency Ebola response
description: Working at Nyakabande Transit Centre (Congolese refugees >200% capacity) and Nakivale Refugee Settlement. Providing PPE, hygiene supplies, psychosocial support. As of 5/28: 1198 cases, >250 deaths in DRC; 7 cases, 1 death in Uganda.

title: EAC convenes emergency health ministers meeting
description: Extraordinary virtual meeting (1-2 June) of East African Community health ministers to coordinate regional response. 9 mobile laboratories deployed at border points, 180+ rapid response experts activated.

title: IOM publishes Multi-Country Situation Report No. 3
description: Covers 26 May-2 June. DRC: 321 confirmed, 116 suspected, 48 deaths. Uganda: 9-15 confirmed. 3.6M IDPs in DRC, 922K in Ituri. Flow monitoring at 8 PoEs shows continued cross-border movement via informal routes.

title: IOM warns Ebola response must cross borders faster than virus
description: Deputy DG Ugochi Daniels: border closures reduce visibility of population movements, undermining screening and surveillance. Calls for mobility-informed response and strengthened cross-border coordination.

title: CEPI commits $60M for Bundibugyo vaccine development
description: Funding to three developers: Moderna, University of Oxford, and Serum Institute of India. Currently in preclinical/early clinical trial stages. No licensed vaccine exists for Bundibugyo strain.

title: Pandemic Fund mobilizes $220.6M emergency financing
description: $175.7M reprogrammed for 9 countries (DRC, South Sudan, Rwanda, Burundi, Tanzania, Zambia, Angola, Kenya, Ethiopia); $44.9M expedited for Uganda, CAR, Republic of Congo. World Bank-hosted fund activation.

title: Africa CDC & WHO launch Continental Strategic Plan ($518M)
description: Six-month plan (June-November) under One Response approach: one plan, one budget, one team. Covers emergency coordination, surveillance, lab testing, IPC, clinical care, community engagement, logistics.

title: Uganda confirms 15 total cases, 4 recoveries
description: DRC: 363 confirmed cases, 62 deaths, 6 discharged. Uganda: 15 confirmed, 1 death, 4 recoveries. 620 contacts monitored, 270 completed 21-day observation. Lab results within ~4 hours.
source: Africa CDC / China.org.cn / African News Agency

title: WHO Director warns of disinformation as second outbreak
description: Dr. Mohamed Janabi: Fighting two outbreaks — Ebola itself and disinformation. Criticized blanket travel restrictions and border closures. Urged screening at official crossings rather than pushing movement to informal routes.
